question: Based on the stacked area chart showing quarterly store operations indicators, which quarter shows the best overall performance?

options.A: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines indicators with different units.
options.B: Q1 shows the best performance because it has the lowest combined values.
options.C: Q2 shows the best performance because it has the highest total area across all indicators.
options.D: Q3 shows the best performance because the indicators appear most balanced.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Revenue, Energy Usage, etc.) into a cumulative area. These metrics have different units and meanings, so adding them together creates a meaningless total. The stacked area cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric analysis is valid.
